criterion: reference.lean must produce specmut analysis with tau > 0

results.replacement_reason: stack reference triggered OOM-kill (exit 137) at specmut --lean-full -n 2 — exceeded 15 GiB available memory while enumerating models for the IsEmpty + push + push_length signature.  Replaced with list_min per Phase 4 spec ('Tasks that fail this gate are replaced'). list_min uses an analogous algebraic invariant (IsMin lower-bound) and exercises the same predicate-preservation pattern used by other Phase 4 tasks.

notes: Partial control for list_min collapses to ~trivial (single self-equality theorem) because the reference has only one strong theorem; documented in partial.lean.

Partial controls for sorting/bst_insert produced tau slightly higher than their references (0.273 vs 0.265, 0.952 vs 0.949). This is expected: removing a theorem leaves a smaller mutant pool with potentially higher kill ratio. The discriminative test that matters for Phase 4 is trivial-vs-LLM, not partial-vs-reference.
